# TingJian - Audio to Text Application

An Android-based offline audio transcription application that supports video audio extraction, speech recognition, and AI-powered summarization.

- Please ensure compliance with local laws and regulations before using this project.

## вњЁ Features

### Core Features
- рџЋҐ **Video Audio Extraction** - Extract audio from various video formats
- рџЋ¤ **Offline Speech Recognition** - Use Vosk engine for transcription without internet
- рџ¤– **AI Smart Summarization** - Support for LLM API integration for text summarization
- рџ“ќ **Result Management** - Copy, save, and share transcription results
- рџ’ѕ **Audio Saving** - Save extracted audio files

### Technical Features
- рџ“± **Material Design** - Modern UI design
- рџ”„ **Real-time Progress** - Display transcription and download progress
- рџ“Љ **Long Audio Processing** - Support for segmented processing of long audio files
- рџЋЇ **Multiple Model Support** - Three different sizes of speech recognition models

### Software Advantages
- рџ”’ **Fully Local** - Speech recognition runs entirely locally, no data uploaded to servers, protecting your privacy
- рџљ« **No Registration Required** - No account registration needed, download and use immediately with no barriers
- рџЋЇ **Ad-Free Experience** - Clean interface with no advertisements, focused on core functionality
- рџ“– **Simple & Intuitive** - Clean and intuitive interface, clear workflow, easy for beginners to get started
- рџ’° **Free to Use** - Core features completely free, no in-app purchases, no subscriptions

### About AI Summarization
- рџ¤– **AI Smart Summarization** - Requires configuration of your own LLM API (supports OpenAI-compatible interfaces)
- рџ”‘ **Your API Key** - You need to provide your own API Key, the app does not collect or upload your key
- рџЊђ **Network Requests** - AI summarization requires internet connection to call LLM services, which may incur API costs
- рџ’Ў **Optional Feature** - AI summarization is optional, not configuring it does not affect the core speech-to-text functionality

## рџ› пёЏ Tech Stack

- **Development Language**: Java
- **Minimum SDK**: Android 5.0 (API 21)
- **Target SDK**: Android 13 (API 33)
- **Speech Recognition**: Vosk Android SDK
- **Audio Processing**: MediaCodec, MediaExtractor
- **Network Requests**: OkHttp
- **UI Framework**: Material Design Components

## рџ“‹ Requirements

### Development Environment
- Android Studio Arctic Fox or higher
- JDK 11 or higher
- Gradle 7.0 or higher

### Runtime Environment
- Android 5.0 (API 21) or higher
- At least 100MB available storage (for models)
- ARM64 architecture devices recommended

## рџљЂ Installation and Building

### Build from Source

#### 1. Clone the Project
```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-username/audio_parse.git
cd audio_parse
```

#### 2. Open Project
- Launch Android Studio
- Select "Open an existing Android Studio project"
- Choose the project root directory

#### 3. Sync Project
- Wait for Gradle sync to complete
- Confirm dependency downloads if prompted

#### 4. Build APK

**Method 1: Build via Android Studio**
1. Click menu `Build` в†’ `Build Bundle(s) / APK(s)` в†’ `Build APK(s)`
2. Wait for build to complete
3. Click the `locate` link in the notification to find the generated APK
4. APK location: `app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`

**Method 2: Build via Command Line**
```bash
# Debug version
./gradlew assembleDebug

# Release version (requires signing configuration)
./gradlew assembleRelease
```

After build completion, APK files are located at:
- Debug version: `app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`
- Release version: `app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk`

#### 5. Install to Device
```bash
# Install via adb
adb install app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk

# Or transfer APK to device directly for installation
```

### Generate Signed APK (for Release)

#### 1. Generate Signing Key
```bash
keytool -genkey -v -keystore audio-parse.keystore \
  -alias audio-parse \
  -keyalg RSA \
  -keysize 2048 \
  -validity 10000
```

#### 2. Configure Signing
Add to `app/build.gradle.kts`:
```kotlin
android {
    signingConfigs {
        create("release") {
            storeFile = file("audio-parse.keystore")
            storePassword = "your_store_password"
            keyAlias = "audio-parse"
            keyPassword = "your_key_password"
        }
    }
    buildTypes {
        release {
            signingConfig = signingConfigs.getByName("release")
            isMinifyEnabled = true
            proguardFiles(
                getDefaultProguardFile("proguard-android-optimize.txt"),
                "proguard-rules.pro"
            )
        }
    }
}
```

#### 3. Build Release Version
```bash
./gradlew assembleRelease
```

## рџ“– User Guide

### First Time Use

1. **Download Speech Model**
   - The app will prompt to download a model on first launch
   - "Small Model" (50MB) is recommended for fast speed and moderate accuracy
   - Other models can be selected in Settings

2. **Configure AI Summarization (Optional)**
   - Go to Settings page
   - Fill in API URL (supports OpenAI-compatible interfaces)
   - Fill in API Token
   - Fill in model name (e.g., gpt-3.5-turbo)
   - Click "Test Connection" to verify configuration

### Basic Operations

1. **Select File**
   - Click "Select File" button
   - Choose video or audio file
   - Supports common formats: MP4, MP3, WAV, M4A, etc.

2. **Start Transcription**
   - Click "Start Transcription" button
   - Wait for audio extraction and transcription to complete
   - Real-time display of progress and results

3. **View Results**
   - Switch between "Transcription Result" and "AI Summary" tabs after completion
   - Click "AI Summary" button to generate smart summary

4. **Save and Share**
   - Copy: Copy text to clipboard
   - Save: Save text to Downloads directory
   - Save Audio: Save extracted audio file
   - Share: Share text via system share function

### Model Information

| Model | Size | Features | Use Case |
|--------|------|----------|----------|
| Small Model | 50MB | Fast speed, moderate accuracy | Quick transcription, daily use |
| Standard Model | 1.3GB | High accuracy, slower speed | Formal occasions, important content |
| Multi-dialect Model | 1.8GB | Supports multiple dialects, highest accuracy | Professional scenarios, dialect recognition |
